sources.issuing_organisation: American Academy of Otolaryngology–Head and Neck Surgery Foundation
sources.exact_document_title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Tonsillectomy in Children (Update)
sources.jurisdiction: United States specialty guideline; international evidence requiring UAE paediatric ENT, anaesthesia, analgesia, and postoperative adaptation
sources.population: Children aged 1 to 18 years being considered for or followed after tonsillectomy.
sources.clinical_setting: Any clinician setting involved in paediatric tonsillectomy assessment, perioperative education, and postoperative outcome review.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for recurrent throat-infection documentation, modifying factors, sleep-related comorbidity context, perioperative education, postoperative pain or bleeding review, and outcome assessment. It does not generate surgery, analgesia, antibiotic, steroid, dose, admission, or discharge instructions.

exact_sections.heading: Key action statements 1–3 — recurrent throat-infection documentation and modifying factors
exact_sections.locator: official fact sheet key action statements 1–3
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting recurrent clinically significant throat infections, episode-associated fever, cervical nodes, exudate or testing, functional impact, and modifying factors without applying an automatic surgery threshold.

exact_sections.heading: Key action statements 4–6 — sleep-disordered breathing and comorbidity context
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting sleep symptoms and selected comorbidity context in children without diagnosing obstructive sleep apnoea or ordering polysomnography.

exact_sections.heading: Key action statements 9, 12, and 15 — pain education, monitoring context, and bleeding outcomes
exact_sections.locator: official guideline key action statements 9, 12, and 15
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports recording clinician-completed postoperative education, pain and hydration context, bleeding outcomes, monitoring, and follow-up. Medicines, doses, admission, discharge, and treatment instructions are excluded.
